items.label: Participants did not try to rush the conversation.
Participants held the conversation at a natural pace.
Participants asked open-ended questions that encouraged participation from the other.
Participants asked questions that allowed the other to share additional information.
Each of the participants took appropriate turns in the conversation.
Both participants contributed to the conversational rhythm.
Participants helped maintain the flow of the conversation.
Participants were able to complete their conversational turns without the other interrupting.
Participants allowed the other to speak without interruption.
Participants were able to pause without being interrupted by the other.
Participants allowed each other to pause.
Participants expressed emotions.
Participants expressed emotions that were acknowledged by the other.
Participants allowed time for the expressions of emotion to be processed.
An external factor (e.g., person, event) interrupted the conversation.
An external factor disrupted the flow of conversation.
Either participant was prevented from completing their conversational turn because of outside factors.
Participants displayed body language that was open to the other.
Participants displayed body language that indicated they were available for the conversation.
Participants were visibly engaged in what the other was saying.
Participants discussed topics unrelated to the medical conversation.
Participants sought to establish rapport by asking questions about the other's personal life or work.
Participants engaged in conversation to get to know each other (outside of clinical roles).
Participants indicated that certain issues could be discussed in the future to have adequate time.
To maintain a deliberate pace, some issues were held for another time.
Participants prioritized a topic to allow for a more deliberate pace.

elements.label: Participants engaged in an unhurried conversation
